name: Barton, Paul Booth
clubs: Skipper; V. M. C. A. Cabinet
description: Whenever you see a great big smile surrounded by several square inches of freckled face and topped off with a thatch of red hair, well that's our own Paul Booth, and you had better be careful in talking with him because he is mighty quick at repartee (although we don't call it that). "Red" is personable, charming, witty, entertaining -- ask any member of the Blacksburg bar set--they know. He is quite an electrical spark and can fix anything from an alarm clock to a "V" cabinet and as a Bible Class leader, we see in him those characteristics which complete the all around man.

name: Williams, Melville Owens Jr.
motto: "Well, well, look what the cat dragged in, 'M. O.' himself."
description: He's from Portsmouth, but he doesn't look that among his honors. When he came to V. P. I. they looked him over and shook their heads in a knowing way, but "M. O." had something either in his head or in his heels and immediately commenced to read his "Rat Bible." He hoped some day to find his name written high on the roster of military immortalists, but alas! Poor "Skipper," "Mell" is the Christian leader of the campus, a man among men, and long will he be remembered in these parts for his noble work. No man on the campus has worked harder than "Mell," and the field of foreign service eagerly awaits him.

name: Barr, Adolph Saucy
description: From a beautiful little city nestled away in the heart of the Lucky mountains of West Virginia there came to Tech a rather young chap. His aspirations, however, were by no means directed toward military, for have the authorities been able to determine him from the rank of private. At first, things seemed strange to him; such cruelty as the Nazis imposed upon him was beyond his understanding. Yet he "stuck it thru" and gained experience that he later found to be valuable in making him the hardest Signaller at V. P. I. After the four years that followed with well-m opportunities overbalancing his mistakes, we find Barr at the end of his college career. We look back and many memories enter our minds that bring gladness to our hearts that he has turned out of us.

name: Ford, John Joseph
description: "DUKE" hails from Southern Virginia and entered Tech with many ideas to reduce military to a minimum. He had a "Skipper's" ambition until his Junior year, when chances were thrown his way and he changed his mind to "Military First." But we are glad to say that he did not have to change his course to achieve his ideal. His outstanding characteristic, which few Tech men have, is that he has not fallen for the "Skipper's Line." He came here to master Applied Biology and has succeeded along with his other work. In all, he was an ambitious student, a consistent worker, a loyal friend and capable officer, characteristics that make a true gentleman. We understand that he is going to continue his studies in medicine.

name: Jack
description: "Jack," like most of us, came to Tech with good resolutions to work hard and make good. But unlike the majority of us, he has kept his resolutions and has made a success of everything that has fallen his lot to do. A strong sense of right and wrong, and a consistency that will not be broken are the qualities that make successful men. "Jack" possesses these. He is a large man, along with many others. To hear the ladies talk about "Jack" one would think him an Adam or a Jack. But no, he is only an ordinary every-day man, possessing a great many qualities that come not only to the ladies, but to the men to admire him. Why is this? There is but one answer. He is interested in people and ever willing to lend a helping hand.

name: Hopkins, Adelbert John Calvin
description: Although "Bull" started at Tech with mixed intentions, ranking corporal of the Band did not satisfy the military instinct which led him to a whole new dimension within him. Consequently, the experiences just a good corporal and "C" Company gained an excellent officer. What he has is very serious experience and manner, which is not the result, however, of experience over his studies, but through Hopkins marks at times are so good as the best, and it is understood of course that he always makes quality credits on the standing correspondence. Being a born discoverer of human nature he makes friends slowly and carefully, but once a friend always a friend, and so we are very glad that "Bull" is a Techman.

name: Baker, John Frazer
motto: "Judge" is not really "Sleepy"; but he has one of those weary looking faces that hides a brain of the earnest type. He can usually be heard to say just before an exam, "Boy, I kinda know my barrels on this stuff," and when the A's are dashed out, Sleepy's name is always in front. No one knows just how to take "Sleepy," with his mysterious surroundings. His personality, however, has been found true on the whole and to warmest well to one of them at least. He is one of the very few who take a big part in the social side of college life as well as the academic side. John was also mentioned for All-American baseball manager.

name: Ligon, Samuel Willis
description: Samuel Willis Ligon maneuvered up to V. P. I. from that fair city of Pamplin, leaving the tender care of home life, to enter into the evils of college life. "Oh, what darling curly hair!" So cries the "weaker" sex. "See that military bearing!" So cried the military department as they handed him his cadetship without mugging. This is not all either, for he seems to manage a high average on his academic work, which seldom goes hand in hand with the above characteristics. "Sam," we say good luck and know that it will mean something to you, as you bring ability to rank so high in all phases of college life should have little trouble with life after college.

name: Beattie, Richard Jackson
clubs: Vice President Head and Hero Club
description: It is sad to make that the time is so near when "Pete" will return to his home in California. He came here last year after studying to stay two, but his personality was such and friends so numerous that we could not let him go. In work and play he has always been an answer to his many friends, and above all to himself. Yes, we feel sure that the world will find in "Pete" those traits which go to make up real manhood and qualities that should be desired by all. "Pete," as he is known to the students, is better known among the far West as "Handsome Pete." From his part heard we feel that his future will be a success.

name: Harper, George Newhill
description: The well-bred young man whose portrait appears herewith is none other than our friend and classmate, George, a good all-around fellow and to know him means as an impression to know him better. He is not only popular with men, for his many friends among the members of the fair sex hold him high in their estimation. He has but two weaknesses: sleep and sleep. Would that there were thirty-six hours in a night; and Burkeville! His thoughts are there. Besides being a scholar, George is one of the highest types of good fellows, always friendly, and greeting everybody with a good-natured smile. He is one of a large class to graduate in Agricultural Engineering and we feel that he will meet with nothing but success.

name: Blanton, John Ellerson
description: Be informed, kind reader, that the stern countenance on this page is not that of the commentator. You see before you the sable and calm "Skinny" Blanton, a man whose very presence is an indispensable factor to the contentment of his friends. Congeniality, his most prominent quality, is of the truest type. He tolerates the frivolities with big-heartedness, and invites the rational with understanding. Nothing is too deep to deprive "Skinny" of a big laugh, nor is anything too trivial to afford him food for thought. In no phase of college life has he triumphantly excelled, but nothing has passed under his observation without being a contribution to his advancement. We know "Skinny" that the world might find him.

name: Hurst, Basil Leonard
description: "Bas" in his numerous and various offices has found no problem too big for his solution. As chairman of the Executive Committee he is a judicious sage; as a captain he is a man who leads not by compulsion but by an irresistible influence; as president of the corps he is the adviser and leader of his fellow students. His wonderful ability to draw into his circle of society and then to so act that each member of his group rejoices because of his presence, can be summed up in one word, personality; and it is this quality that has drawn to "Bass" his many friends. And we predict that because of this ability he shall have the best in "Fair" quality of future success and happiness.

name: Butler, Howard Wade
motto: "Speech may be silver but Silence is GOLD."
clubs: First Sergeant, Company "B"; German Club; A.S.C.E. (1922-23)
Major, Staff; Secretary-Treasurer, German Club; Sub-Executive Committee; Social Activities Committee; A.S.C.E. (1923-24)
description: Though an old and well-known proverb and probably considered a mere relic by our present generation of strong self-assertion, it is obviously true that Wade has found the "happy medium" that is worth its weight in gold. Things that we fear we doubt, but seeing is believing. In Wade we have found a self-reliant young man of strong will power and pursuing an integrity of purpose that we all admire. Nor do these qualities impress upon him that impenetrable dignity that looks off the world's friend. And though there is dignity, beneath it we find a sincere and jolly friend with a disposition that we can truthfully call infallible.

name: Easley, Robert Marshall
description: Yes, the boy with all those stripes is the "Staff" from South Boston. He was the brains of the electrical wizards and a right-hand man to Claudius, with the ability to wire anything from a grocery store to a railroad. No one kept track of him during the summers of '21 and '22, but the summer of '23 found him at Middle Desert actively engaged in shoveling sand in preparation to become an officer in Uncle Sam's future army. When it came to getting out of work he had no rival, for wherever there was a working detail he could be found at some shady spot about a quarter of a mile away. "The Staff" was a standout at the R.O.T.C. dance and shook a wicked Baltimorean hand.

name: Brown, Leonard Lee
description: He's good natured and big-hearted and kind & lazy; but still he'd make a spanking good husband. Many of the lasses ask before this time and all hope that he has to carry a club to keep them away from him. He believes in life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness; and from his ready smile and his humorous speech, we believe that he has found all these. "Brownie" has made many friends in college in spite of the fact that he has lived in town, and he will long be remembered by them for his generosity, his freedom from false pride, and his jolly good friendship.

name: Carrier, Carl Hudson
description: Were it not for Carl we often wonder what would become of the motorcyclists around Blacksburg, for he is a master of all the intricacies of the little two-wheeled machine. However, his skill is not confined to the mechanical world alone. He is always found looking an excellent hand in Cupid's game of chance, but from all indications we have some doubt as to where his heart lies at present. Never think for a moment that Carl spends all his time in this way. He is a good student, a loyal friend, a perfect gentleman, and is never so happy as when helping someone out of difficulty. All that can be said about a "man's man" can be said of Carl. Sketch: Fallen Angel.

name: Burns, Charles Leon
description: "Bobbie" came to V.P.I. after spending a year at both Bridgewater and Carnegie Tech. "Bobbie" has a great capacity for work; not only does he hold a high place in his class, but he has also shown great ability in V.M.C.A. work. Nevertheless, he has found time to take part in social affairs, and nothing pleases him so much as to be able to help a fellow student. Judging from numerous letters that are always to be found in the mail, certain members of the fair sex are as much interested in this young man as his classmates are. Those of us who will later return to the high places in life may see him again, but the rest may now bid him goodbye, and regret to lose his companionship. "Bobbie" has made his mark and will not be forgotten.

name: Gregory, Lanwood Eugene
clubs: Private Company "A" A. S. M. E.
Private Company "A" A. S. M. E. Skipper: Mason
description: One September evening when the "Huckleberry" came steaming into Blackburg, it had among its passengers a lad who, though quite young and timid-looking at that time, has grown into a real Tech man. His excellent record as a student shows that few, if any, surpass him in ability. His fine sense of honor, loyalty and fairness, his high ideals and his clean, wholesome character, his kindly attitude toward men have given him a position of esteem and admiration. So—let's be fair and tell all that he deserves and is admired by some friends of the fair campus. But in all seriousness, now, the least that we can say for "Skinny" is that he's a man for it.

name: Clark, John Niles
description: From the beginning of his college career we could see that Johnny was born for success at Tech. His genuine upright qualities and unshakable good humor, together with his good judgment have won for himself esteem and respect by all those who know him. Much might be said in his behalf, but the honors bestowed upon him by his classmates prove his worth to us. He has the distinction of being Business Manager of the best Virginia Tech that has ever been published at V. P. I. and much of this success is due to his tremendous efforts in procuring funds for this organization. Aside from all this, and being Secretary and Treasurer of the Cadillac Club, "Johnny" finds time to indulge in our "hoo-dah" meetings and contributes greatly by his presence.

name: Ewing, Charles Wischford
clubs: Corporal Company "D," Secretary and Treasurer, Richmond Club (1921/22)
description: Dear pals, let us for a moment while we sing the praises of the gallant youth whose picture you see here. Who would imagine in this handsome young man the bashful boy who came to dwell among our boy friends years ago? Charlie has too many good traits to mention in the short space we have, but we must call to mind his one great weakness—good-looking girls—let that be not a bad trait after all. He shines not only with the girls, but also in his academic grades, which are always excellent. We cannot quite understand the last, though, for he truthfully said that he was never known to study. Charlie is liked by everyone wherever he goes, and we know that he will be successful in whatever he attempts in life.

name: Clark, Green Staples
description: Among that silent throng who invaded the stronghold of the college yesterday, Jack, or "Jack," was this stalwart, clear-eyed, black-haired youth. He hailed from the Magic City and in numerous fronts had made him a wizard at one thing at least, for this young man can at once put the "black joker" on jamborees, and conjuring the leader's good intentions, called down in numberless acts the choicest of homely Sunday banter, a la home-cooked. Jack was an uninterrogated guest by military halls of fame. "U.S." achieved success in numerous other activities; his honor is well evident of the fact. His outstanding characteristics are his fair play, clear memory and common sense, to which are added dedication and skill.

name: Herman, Lesher Pitchard
clubs: Business Manager V. M. C. A. Band
Y. M. C. A. Editor Virginia Tech
Advisory Board Y. M. C. A.
description: Early one September morning, a stalwart man of the "City by the Sea," stepped from the "Hackleberry," and stood for a moment looking at the mountains which were to surround him during his last years at Tech. His life tightened with a determination to make good, and true to the old saying, "Time will tell," he has made good as shown by the esteem in which he is held by a host of friends. Honors were not just handed out to him, but have come as an honest reward for his endeavors. We are proud to have known Herman, proud to have called him our classmate and friend.

name: Coleman, John Franklin
motto: "You can take a boy out of the country, but you can never take the country out of a boy."
description: However, here is the exception. Unusual circumstances kept "Frank" from returning immediately following his graduation, so he joined us when we were Sophomores. Strong of will power, he is yet an easy mark for the opposite sex. If anything comes his way, woman will be it, as he does not realize that the "woman was made for the happiness of man, she has disturbed the tranquility of the world." Words cannot express our thoughts for such a fine Southern gentleman and true gentleman. Thus his gentle personality and charming character he has won for himself the highest regard of such and every one of us, and we are confident that he will always bring glory to his Alma Mater and honor to the class.

name: Teng-Liang, Jie
description: Seeking knowledge, this enterprising lad from the Orient crossed the Pacific in 1929, and upon entering the Golden Gate, was guided by his dream for a military career to V.P.L. However, on months at this institution studied that dream, and finding wise advice, he came to V.P.L. to transport. In character, Jeng is a gentleman, generous and loyal marked, having lofty ideals and a great personality that will win him friends wherever he goes. Believing in doing things in proportion, "Ting Ling" never let his studies interfere with his college life. However, he has developed wonderful ability as an Electrical Engineer. His highest ambition is to turn the wild and destructive forces of the mighty Yangtze and Yellow rivers into power that will dominate all China.

name: Cormack, William White
description: Because of his six feet three inches of slim and lanky frame, "Slim" is one of the outstanding men of V.P.I. We find him at his best in the mining group, where he has learned the art of "gold-digging" to perfection. "Slim" has spent four years among us, with an interruption of one year during which time he took a course in "tree dancing." As exchange editor of The Virginia Tech for two years, "Slim" became known through his many humorous articles. We are not sure but what some of these found their way into the heart of a certain lass in Hampton. For it was during the maneuvers at Fort Monroe in the summer of 1923, that a little girl of Hampton last wrote to the heart of our friend "Slim."

name: Daugherty, Lyman Carroll
description: "L. C." came to us from Chesterfield County. His greatest desire is to become a teacher of Agriculture, and if he is as capable in teaching as he has been in learning, the world will soon be given another great man. Aside from his studies, "L. C." has found time to mingle with the crowd and is greeted with a hearty welcome wherever he goes. "L. C." is a quiet chap, yet when one knows him well, he discovers a depth of nature that is surprising. Judging from the amount of mail he gets, it will not be long after June before this young man takes unto himself a better half. It has been a pleasure to know and associate with this man and we are all expecting big things from him in the future.

name: Calam, William Edward
description: A chap thrown down the steps, dragging heavy books, a lot of stuff, and you just know that it's "Ed" Calaham, always good-natured, taking life easy, and having a good time even if his name does appear quite frequently in the "Evening News." He always makes a quick recovery after a fall, and has a mighty silly way of looking at things. But when it comes to athletics his whole heart and soul are in it; and the varsity will have a valuable baseball player in June. In the spring a young man's fancy, but it's always spring for "Ed." When it comes to women, well, nobody else has a chance. As an electrical engineer, we are expecting great things of him. He is a friend and is solid then and there.

name: Francis, Lee McClung
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Student Branch A.I.E.E.
description: "Mac" must have heard that old saying that "A man is not without honor save in his own country." Not many years ago there was a party, and he drifted over the mountains to work. Some and sometimes it took. These years have surely rolled by, and we now find Mac without the foreign, but with his own firmly established. Few of us had ever heard of the little town of Lexington before we met "Willie," but before the first year was over we learned that there were more times guarded a spot famous for scholars, soldiers, and gentlemen. During these four years "Willie" has shown himself to be an excellent example of these qualities, and it is with earnest regret that we tell his story.

name: Cooke, Harold Lee
description: "Cookie," a "Fallen Angel," is one of our most diligent "Sky pilots." Of the usual type we know him to be free, though he has one little girl he would travel the world over, Mary Anne, his caring sister, of which he is chief support, has asked, we fear, once too often in an attempt to find another "sky pilot." Now his chief burdens are writing letters and has to obtain paper, stamps, and his text books. "Cookie" is known to every one of us as a regular guest in our home, and he is pictured here as he must naturally appear out from the woods, where it is rumoured he is building a lot of new things which will some day when he can study writing in the heart of the Wilderness of Virginia.

name: Wallace, Harry Moore Jr.
description: "Quiz" was his nickname because of frequent "skating" together with the similarity of his features and those of a certain well-known golfer. When we wanted a characteristic picture of "Quiz," our minds naturally pictured a mass of wire, wood, copper, screws, and other inconceivable apparatus that goes to make up a radio set. Because of his quiet nature we have not been able to obtain much data on his relations with the local set. However, when we think of the combination of his moonlighting on the key panel with his sixteen-inch tires and the dainty letters he gets, we doubt that he is lacking along that line. "Quiz" always has a cheerful greeting for everyone he meets, and he is known to all, from the quiet senior to the greatest "rat."

name: Mills, William Darden, Jr.
description: Commonly heard in the "Evening News," "M. W. Darden, absent civics." There's a reason, too. His prayer, "God, give sleep to the bad, so that the good may be undisturbed," is answered every day. As to the interpretation of this, we have to see. Being fond of walking and kept in practice by "agricultural hunting," he decided to do that immense circuit that runs in and from the "Aggie Hall." Not in vain either, for he is now one of our A. E. writers, long anxious to impart his knowledge to future generations. With his wonderful faculty for making friends, he has extended himself to as well as to countless members of the "weaker" sex.

name: Jackson, John Paul
clubs: Vice President, A. S. C. E.
description: Instead of pursuing a course in Civil Engineering, we feel sure that the course most suited him, because he's never been known to study, yet he's one of the leaders in Civil. "Johnny Paul" had not been with us long before we found that there were other comrades more attractive than Civil Engineering. But he is not to blame, 'cause every boy is susceptible to that little member. Paul is just the kind of a boy that every one likes to call friend, and always will be, as he is remembered for his straightforwardness and sincerity.

name: Day, Philip Baldwin, Jr.
motto: "Ready, aim, fire!"
description: No, this is not Israel Putnam, but none other than Philip B. Day, our range officer. An unerring eye is his gift and the bullseye matters when Pat lines his sights upon the target. All great men make mistakes and he has his love affairs, each succeeding one eclipsing the one preceding. We have here a true agriculturist who loves to talk, and talk of love. We know not what the future holds, but we prophesy that "Pat" will some day hold limitless public plantations in South America—the ones owned by the Goodyear Rubber Company.

name: Willey, John Penny
description: Still writing, and we can imagine how the fair young damsel will succumb to the eloquent flow of words. Three letters a day and the special on Sunday—this was enough for her wild eyes! Willie—this may be his name, but there's no question about it when he's around the gate. A handsome young man with wavy hair was enough to attract even the commissary's attention, yet Willie now does the chores of laundry, sewing, medicine, dentistry, because of the war, and even the machine has at times been painting while in the ranks. He's minded, but in the end he always returns to his cherished surroundings.

name: Denson, Alfred Meade
description: "Dirk," gentle readers, is a white man in every respect, all wood and a yard wide. The professor seeks the radium when Denson enters their classroom. Ready and capable at any and all times to help the other fellow, he has made a multitude of friends among his fellow students. Frequent tardiness and sleeping are his long suits. Nothing bothers him, not even military. "Dirk" worked in the Bethlehem Steel Plants for a while, but he decided that the long hot days of North Carolina were more attractive, and consequently he turned his steps southward to Raleigh. We know not what line of chemistry this man intends to follow, but we feel sure that he will make a success in whatever line he chooses. Englewood, a suburb of Norfolk, Virginia, though not on the map at the present time, will be when "Dirk" turns his genius loose on the world.

name: Bryant, Litch Strother
clubs: Private Company "H": Masonic Club: Chemical Club; Richmond Club: Rifle Club: Wrestling: Sunat (1922-23)
Private Company "H": Masonic Club: Chemical Club; Richmond Club: Skipper (1923-24)
description: Let us introduce the only and original tea-bond at V. P. L., for if you want a cup of tea all you have to do is to see "Strangler" and a cup of her charming beverage is quickly yours. Although not one of the shining stars on the wrestling squad, Bryant brought back an incredible nickname from the mat. On entering Tech he revealed his intention of finishing in Chemistry, and after four long years of patient study has accomplished this purpose. Tech will now lose a "Skipper," but the world will gain a man.

name: Lee, Walton Finks, Jr.
motto: "Whammy a cigarette! How about getting one for the Lady? No, I can't start studying until the whistle blows," can be heard nearly every night in front of Plank and Hugo's by none other than Lee Walton Finks, Jr.
description: Lee Walton's only objection to military being that it is prohibited to wear blue neckties. He acquired a Lieutenant's rank to grace his Senior year. This, however, is not the only phase of college activities in which he has excelled, as a glance at his list of honors will show that his abilities were at least in part recognized while he has been here at Tech. Also, from the manner in which he has done things interesting to him, we feel very little uncertainty as to his future.

name: Whitlock, Lyndon Johnson Jr.
description: Introducing Lyndon Johnson Whitlock, Jr., of Milton, N. C. Yes, he is a "Tin Head," and under pressure, will admit it. He came to me in the fall of '28 with ambitions of becoming greater than himself. However, at the beginning of his junior year the electrical world lost its employee, and he began to prepare himself for the medical position or President of some large corporation. He holds the local sleeping record of 16 hours and 47 minutes, and during the morning are his accomplishments. Awake, he interprets the waves of the Troposphere, and his dimples make him one of the reasons that girls leave home. His ability, willingness to work (when necessary) and a pleasant personality assure us that his will be a successful future.

name: French, William Hampton
description: The real story not before it, this young man from the wilds of Callow, Va., has been named by one, if not, as being the handsome man at V.P.I. In his junior year he took a sudden leap into the world of military and became a sergeant, but the picturesque life of a "skagga" proved too strong an attraction and military lost another ambitious general. In addition to being a soldier in all his classes and a skilled amateur in English, "Hampton" has taken the opportunity for the Underwood typewriter. But we have often wondered why he chose Underwood when Hampton (Va.) has such a potent attraction for him. "Hampton's" cheerful good nature and readiness to help any one every one has won him a host of friends at Tech and will carry him far on the road to success.

name: Taylor, William Chamberlayne
motto: "The pen is mightier than the sword,"
description: Behold, "Baby Bill," the only one of his kind in captivity, who has been confined within the walls of V.P.I. for the last four years, and having acquired sufficient polish of civilization, we think that he may be allowed to stroll forth into the world without his nurse and guardian. "The best of goods is often done up in the smallest package," says an ancient proverb, and to this we must attribute his success with the ladies. Daily, winged words of eloquence and love flow forth from the gilded point of his pen. He hopes to become a successful engineer and if he does not reach his goal, then "cherchez la femme."

name: Graham, Samuel Oscar
clubs: Northern Virginia and D. C. Club
description: "Sam" has made wonderful strides in football since coming to us, but no one would have thought he would have made such a star from the way he started his "red" game. In fact, it is said that all during that year he was never known to leave his room except to go to the library during his leisure hours. "Sam's" real football life started the fall of his Sophomore year. He started his course of study here in Mechanical Engineering, but at the beginning of his Sophomore year changed over to Agriculture to specialize in Agronomy. We still believe that he has missed his calling for it is said that he can buy goods from a Scotchman and sell it to a Jew at a profit.

name: Rea, Robert James
description: Those who know "Bob" have found him to be one of the hardest workers in his class. Whenever there is work to be done and a clear thinking man needed, he can always be relied upon to do his part. His disposition is one that is rarely found; always ready to give a helping hand and a cheerful smile to those around him. "Bob's" interest in activities in college has been very noticeable. His value as a football player made him indispensable to the team during his four years at VPI. Besides being prominent in football, he has made the Senior class a good and capable president during his last year in college. "Bob" is quite interested in the property arts, as most men are; consequently, he expects to grow "younger" when he finishes his education.

name: Hall, Lewis Augustus
description: Some people call it the "garden spot," but "Luke" calls it home. And what do I Norfolk and me? Another "Ruf" who was cast upon the "G." The Commandant appointed him a corporal, but after hearing him call "Wits on fourth floor," for nine months, he was left in the ranks during his junior year. Santa Claus hated to see so many good men left behind; so Lieutenant strips were given him at Christmas this year. For two years "Luke" has been one of the main stars of the Tech Orchestra, and is manager and director this year. Although an Eastern Virginian, it is said that a Southwestern Virginian has a hold on him. His high ideals and his appreciation of high music has brought him success in Tech.

name: Roberts, Arthur Jr.
description: When September, 1920, brought us to Blacksburg, "Artie" didn't look different from the rest of us. It was not long, however, before his high sense of honor, his congenial disposition, and his intent to climb the ladder of fame was realized. Although a little light, he set out in his "Bats" year to make a "V.P." in football and after four years of hard work he received that which was due him. The military department got an eye on him with the result that he became the commanding officer of the Second Battalion. He was popular among the weaker sex as well as with his classmates, made an excellent officer, and was as true a friend as a man ever had.

name: Hallatt, Henry Mariano
description: This young man chose Dairy Husbandry as his life's work, his chief ambition being to start a revolution in Mexico; not a military one, for he aspires to no military honors, but one far nobler, namely, that of promoting the dairy industry of his native country. We wonder why he ever selected Dairying, for he admits that he has never milked a cow. He is a favorite among the ladies and is known far and near as can be readily noticed by observing the various postmarks on his numerous letters. Just where his heart is we do not know. His popularity among the ladies is paralleled only by that among his fellow students, for to meet him is to be impressed by his winning personality.

name: Jackson, Lloyd Dilwin
clubs: Secretary Treasurer A. S. M. E.
description: Camp says this young man's picture and you will not wonder why we call him "Deacon." He began his "Rat" life in the fall of 1920 by delivering a sermon in the local Shenandoah Valley Club. Now, four years have passed and he is still the same "Deacon." In Jack we have a true gentleman. He is a friend to all and is always ready to help those in need. His hard work coupled with natural ability has placed him among the leaders of his class, Mechanical Engineers. On account of "Deacon's" quiet nature we have been able to secure but small evidence of his efforts with the club, but it is reported from good sources that he will embark upon the study of metallurgy sometime after leaving us in June.

name: Hitt, Norman Eily
description: Behold "Shrimp" - another product of the "Garden Spot of the East." Our hero, sixteen inches of concentrated pep and energy. His entry into the purring profession will usher in a new style of abbreviated uniform or a new use for stilts, we know not which. "Shrimp" is another name for military sergeants known as "Skippers," and a loyal one, too. It is told on him that when he was first detailed as "O.G." the commandant made him wear a bayonet instead of a saber, because he would have made too much noise dragging the latter up and down stairs. The ladies think him quite nice, his classmates know him for a good man and a true friend.
text: Behold "Shrimp" - another product of the "Garden Spot of the East." Our hero, sixteen inches of concentrated pep and energy. His entry into the purring profession will usher in a new style of abbreviated uniform or a new use for stilts, we know not which. "Shrimp" is another name for military sergeants known as "Skippers," and a loyal one, too. It is told on him that when he was first detailed as "O.G." the commandant made him wear a bayonet instead of a saber, because he would have made too much noise dragging the latter up and down stairs. The ladies think him quite nice, his classmates know him for a good man and a true friend.

name: Harnshorger, Humphrey Peake
description: There is a man below us, but he is miles and miles of altitude, with another eleven buildings in the sky, and a handsome superstructure high among the clouds. As the passing crowd gaze with admiration at the towering skyscraper, we the last ones give up our "HiP". Yet others along the street glance at the bulk of "HiP's" attention, for Mother Electricity has claimed him as her ally and we cannot leave our "HiP's" most amazing qualities, his friendly smile and his willingness to pass his superior knowledge on to others. "HiP" expects to earn his education at Cornell University.

name: Ballenge, Howard
motto: "Hey, Jack, gimme a cigarette."
clubs: Private Company "F", Northern Virginia and D. C. Club
Private Company "E", Northern Virginia and D. C. Club
description: Jack, thus awakened, usually sighs bravely and supplies "Squeekum" with the required Lord Chesterfield. We now see the villain's deep scheme in teaching Jack the habit our "rat" year, and extend our brotherly sympathies to the victim. Even Sly Jack could not have had such foresight. The success "Squeak" seems to have its results when the "lemons" are concerned as is evidenced by the complaints that "Squeekum" never tires of telling us about. However, we like, especially after the sixth repetition, but our gentle hearts are of no avail and he squeaks on and we endure. As a title we suggest M. R. F. (Master of Buck Faggeno) which is not by any means the least of his accomplishments, though it creates less disturbance to our peace of mind.

name: Kincaid, Rhea Thomas
clubs: Lee, West and Scott Country Club
description: Out of the mountains of Lee County comes "Tin Can" with all the wisdom and humor that is characteristic of the mountaineers of Virginia. The stripes of the military have never invaded a place in his mind; he is his home, yet we find him a loyal "Skipper." The profession of Animal Husbandry changed him, and he possesses every talent to make him successful in his profession. Kindness, friendliness and frankness are his qualities which make him friends wherever he goes. To go along with him is his gift of talkativeness. Stay with him five minutes and you feel like you have known him for years.

name: Pittenger, Melville Chambers
description: "Pitt" came to Tech four years ago with his first suit of long trousers, since then he has changed from a boy to a real man, and we are proud of him. He stands high in the class and we will never forget him as a noble "Skipper." He doesn't talk so much, but when he does, he says a lot in a few words. Along with his college work he hasn't lost sight of the value of the fair sex. He has never had less than three on the string, and once we recall right. It looks now as though he will settle in Lee country with one of the three. He is a real Tech man, and we are sure that after he goes out into life he will make good as he has done here.

name: Lockwood, Earl Lewis
description: Hailing from "The Garden State of the East," sometimes called Norfolk, Earl has always lived by the sea shore, but he makes the mountains of Roanoke his stepping place on all his work and journeys. Electrical Engineering is his chosen profession, and judging from the interest he takes in electrical subjects, the success of Siemens will have a worthy rival when Earl begins to practice his profession. "Sleepy" early chose the ranks as his place in the military department and has at last attained his goal as a member of the "Champers." "Sleepy" is another of those lads whom you have to know well before you can fully appreciate his sterling qualities. He is a conscientious worker, a loyal friend, and a man well liked by all who know him.

name: Fentress, Robert Wood
description: "Sir, Woods will you show me how to prove that S. M. L. equals S. O. L. in this problem?" This is just one of the many S. O. L.'s with which Woods was besieged because he was recognized as one of the best math scribes in our class. He was not selfish, but would help anyone who came to him for aid, and the unfortunate fairly flocked to his room. Woods had no military aspirations, a fact for which we are truly thankful, for undoubtedly he would have been the highest of officers. As it was he ranked high among the non-going members of our class, a real "Skipper." We do not know where Woods intends to follow his chosen profession as a Mechanical Engineer, but we understand that the warm climate down in Georgia has a strong attraction for him.

name: Mahaney, John Philip
clubs: Sergeant Company "B", A. S. M. E. (1922-23)
Lieutenant Company "B", A. S. M. E. (1923-24)
description: This is not to introduce John Philip Mahaney but to recall to Technion a picture of that "curly-haired scrawny" buried in the depths of his studies. "Haney," as he is known to Technion, has been one of our leading students for the past four years. He is constantly trying to better a way to accomplish his tasks and we predict that some day his methods will prove valuable to engineering. The ambitious spirit of this young Technion would never allow him to lead the currier life of a student. He left the ranks to become an engineer, and for his services in this capacity he was awarded a certificate his senior year. Tech may lose Haney, but she gives to the world an engineer, whose training and noble character will be a constant source of pride to his "alma Mater."

name: Morgan, Benjamin Thomas
description: We are inclined to owe one of those thriving homes in the Piedmont section of Virginia for playing among us one of its products in the form of a neat looking chap known as Tom Morgan. On first acquaintance one may not be impressed, but now the veil of Tom's modesty is broken, there is revealed an unbridled supply of pride and we can well remember the characteristics that Tom has displayed in times of discouragement and disappointment. His departure will leave many sad hearts among his friends, but we are comforted by the fact that there are many on the "outside" who have a place in their hearts which will surely be filled by one who loves to serve others.

name: Mckee, Paul Dulaney
description: 'P. D.'s first year at V. P. I. was successful, but being demoted to corporal in his second year he had the great distinction of giving the corps an extra hour of sleep, yet the commissary did not approve of his kindness and he was raised to the rank of a private. A good cuckatoo, a poor student, yet he did graduate at the top of his class. 'P. D.' expects to spend a fifth year here and the reason is on the Hill. In '21 'P. D.' won honor for himself and for the school in the Stock Judging Contest at Atlanta. His ability to judge the fair was as great as his ability to judge animals, we would like to be the future Mr.

name: Korson, Charles Harmon
description: "SCRIBE" because of his frank disposition and unpretentious ability, is one of the most popular men in the class of '21. The ladies say he is handsome, and they must be right. At any rate "SCRIBE" possesses some kind of magnetism, manifested either by his smile or permanent wave that wins them. "SCRIBE" says that a good location of work and play is a good rule to follow. Best of all, he lives up to this standard, being one of the best students in his class. Besides being efficient in other activities, he is a leader of initiative and sound judgment. We have always found "SCRIBE" to be a gentleman, a sportsman, a loyal and generous friend and we feel sure that in his chosen profession he will carry his way to the heights of success.

name: Parks, Harry Roberts
clubs: Vice President A. S. A. K.
description: "Peter" first came to Tech in 1919, but being a victim of the "Flu" in 1920, he was forced to stay out a year. He has become quite an artist at bridge and has hundreds, but his favorite pastime seems to be sleeping. He seldom works hard, but when he does, he puts his whole soul into it and always comes out successful. Different from most of us, he has never allowed the ladies to cause him much worry; however, there is a certain one at Abingdon who seems to have captured his heart. When you first meet "Peter," you are likely to form a wrong opinion of him because of his apparent quietness. Yet when you know him, you admire and respect him for his steadiness and patience, which accounts for his reserved manner.

name: Falzon, John Jr.
description: There came to Tech four years ago one of the hardest working members of the class of '24, namely, "Doc" Falzon. Since early in his "cut" year he has been one of the "sharks" and "racketeers" of the Corps. During that time he has had girls, and dates, and dates, and girls. Blacksburg, Radford, Abingdon, and other nearby towns have been the scenes of his conquests, but at present Christiansburg seems to hold "the attraction." "Doc" chose as his profession Civil Engineering, and we are sure that he will make good as he has the ability and the grit to put things across. To know "Doc" is to like him, and many are the happy memories that he will leave in the minds of his classmates.

name: Gwynn, David Franklin Jr.
motto: "UNCONSCIOUS"
description: As the Nile was to Anthony, so the New River is to Gwynn. Many times has he crossed its turbid waters to find peace and happiness in Radford. He has sent in his pockets, smokes Camel Cigarettes and smokes 'em wherever he goes as the mail bears testimony. In his younger days he was a military soldier, having lost more squads than any other corporal at V. P. L. He was also a sergeant, but at the end of last year he was retired to private life and is now a member of the Royal Order. David is a very promising young electrical engineer, and if the fair sex will let him alone we predict a great future for him in his chosen field of interest and endeavor.

name: Sasser, Reverdy Grisekin
motto: "Revelle"
description: "Revelle" has been noted throughout his stay at Tech for his lack of military ambitions, and he is therefore a first class "Skipper." When there is any fun to be had, Sasser is right there; and if there isn't, he entertains himself with soliloquy, at which game he shuffles a wicked deck. He is also a football player of distinction and during the past season was one of the mainstays of the "Suicides." Sasser has yet to fall for feminine wiles, but we predict that when he does he is going to fall hard. Although he has not overburdened himself with studying, he has nevertheless succeeded in "writing" on Geology, and we may expect to hear of his discovery of some of the largest oil fields of the future.

name: Hagy, James
motto: "Here's to Jim, the lady-killer, who does not part his hair in the middle nor assume the drag of the cake-waiter. To his name that carries the power of attraction of a killer. We speak of his fickleness, yet when the grades are posted, he has nothing to regret. His willingness and congeniality make him a good miner and we predict for him a great future. His hopes lie in the coal fields of West Virginia where he expects to apply the principles of coal mining that he has successfully learned here, and we expect to hear of him as a leading mining engineer."

name: Williams, Roy Marshall
description: This energetic, blue-eyed lad came to V.P.I. from the state of Georgia. Roy is one of those Tech men who, on a short visit to the Eastern part of the state, discovered that it is better to write to many rather than write a bit just to one. As a room orderly, he sets the standards that might well be published as a model. His selection of pictures that walls his den are known to all that visit him. His outstanding feature (unlike most men) is an inclination to work. He is very much interested in the profession that he has chosen, and perhaps some day we will again hear of him as a noted "juice pusher." We have found him, as we expected, tried and true and above all, a friend worth having. To know Roy is to admire him and to have a true friend always.

name: Moore, Charles Guthrie
clubs: First and Chief Varsity of Skippers
description: Your lad from 'Up North' (Pulaski), and after attending the A.T.C. at the University of Virginia for one year, he entered Tech. Being militarily inclined he rose rapidly in the ranks, first a Corporal, then to a Sergeant, and finally to the much coveted grade of 'Skipper.' And a true 'Skipper' could not be found. As a baseball star there have been few at Tech to outshine 'Gus.' He has experienced many times the shock received when a long run was stopped. In fact, he liked the experience these shocks gave him so well that in order to become familiar with other shocks, he joined the course of Electrical Engineering. He was one of the most friendly and cheerful characters at Tech and ever in the minds of all Techies an all-round 'Gus' being laughed with.

name: Wyatt, Harry Caswell
clubs: Private Company "B" - A. I. E. E., Skipper
description: This young man, better known to us as "Peachy," came to us from Greenbrier Military Academy. At prep school "Peachy" was reported as getting on everything that he undertook, even skating in military. The military knowledge he gained was of inestimable value at Tech, for by this knowledge he was able to aid himself at a high ranking corporation which had been third upon him, and this led to his esteemed membership in "The Royal Order of Skippers." Having been in electrical locomotive work, "Peachy" became greatly concerned about the boy and girl of distribution and determined to find out all about it from Tech's electrical course. Of course we know that we shall hear of him in electric railway some time, somewhere.

name: Minter, Lewis Spiller
description: Lewis, better known as "Sphenoid" from his connection with Dr. Hubley's Crystallography, is taking Mining Engineering, and some day we expect him to discover a big gold mine that will make people forget the "Rush of 1848." He seems to be one of the commandant's chosen few, never having failed to get an invitation from the Major to go "Squirrel Hunting" at the end of each month. Lewis is one of the lucky few who have fallen for more than an education since he came to Blacksburg. Indeed, he declares that an education is of more importance when compared with the other benefits derived since coming to this burg. Therefore, we offer you this picture as representing his most characteristic pose. We feel that if the world gets a chance to know Lewis as we do, his success will be assured.

name: Winstead, Aubrey Ewell
motto: "Say, 'weary,' do you understand this problem?" "Well, a little perhaps,"
description: and so saying he would lay down his work and enlighten the one in trouble. The above is almost a daily occupation and is typical of Winstead. He is always ready to help anyone out except the Military Department, and thus we are justified in saying that he is a most excellent student, true friend and a good "Skipper." Aubrey has a weakness that is known to us all: arguing. The only thing that keeps him out of that land of dreams is an argument, and then he will often pile up on his "May" to argue. Aubrey has his heart set on South America and from all indications he will get there and meet success with it.

name: Nixde, Thomas Sanford Dunaway
clubs: Sergeant Company "A," Vice-President Rappahannock Valley Club, Chemical Club, Lee Literary Society (1922-23)
Captain Company "E," President Rappahannock Valley Club, Chemical Club, German Club, Lee Literary Society (1923-24)
description: Who is the new military authority in our midst? It is none other than Captain Nixde of "E" Company. "Sam," as he is known to his classmates, takes great pride in his company and is always working to perfect it. But this is only one of his interests. In his chosen profession, Chemical Engineering, Sam is quite adept. Of course, when one is writing about Sam one must bring up the subject of the eternal bachelor. T. S. D. insists that he is going to be a bachelor; nevertheless, he is seen quite often writing to the opposite sex. As a center on "A" Company's basketball team for three years Sam has added one more level to his charm. Taking him all in all, Sam is quite a fine, likeable "old chap."

description: "Julian C."—the C doesn't stand for Carrot, though it should as "Cotton" is simply a pun. Again that "C" might well stand for Chemical, in which line he surely shines. Roden came to Tech from "John Marshall," determined to live up to the high reputations made by his three brothers who graduated here in the past. And he has certainly succeeded. Though one of the busiest and hardest working members of our class, he could always find time to help the other fellow.

name: Noble, Aubrey Joseph
description: Here he is, Aubrey Joseph Noble of Richmond! Don't crowd, girls; he will take care of you one at a time. I warn you though, Cleopatra would have been easy in his hands. It's his hair! Never since Adam decided to take a breathing spell has mortal man been adorned with such curly locks as ornament our hero's brow. The English Department and the dictionary were short-circuited his "Bat" year, but the imitative talent until English was buried, and the chief mourners were none other than Aubrey himself. Now he contents himself with Noah Webster's masterpiece. For four years "A. J." has proved an indispensable asset to the minstrels. This year he reached the apex of his career, winning the subjugation of "Dream Boy." In spite of these virtues, his congeniality and willing temper, combined with his wit have won him a place of distinction among us.

name: Williams, Frank Miles
motto: "Hey! You with the 'Vic' under your arm! What's your name?"
clubs: Corporal Company "F," Cotillion Club; Mecklenburg Club (1921-22)
First Sergeant Company "F"; Second Vice-President Cotillion Club; Vice-President Junior Class; President Junior Senior Dance; Sub-Executive Committee; Mid-Virginia Club (1922-23)

Captain Company "F"; Vice-President Senior Class; Leader Cotillion Club; Business Staff 1924 Bugle; Social Activities Committee; Mid-Virginia Club; Vice-President Corps; Chairman, Sub-Executive Committee (1923-24)
description: Thus Frank came to V. P. I. to study Electrical Engineering. However, he realized such a severe shock after his Sophomore year that he decided to discard his Elements of Electricity and began to collect a library on "How to Run the Sale." He has become quite proficient in this in spite of his frequent committee meetings, military obligations and trips to "Columbia." His striking personality has made him something of a social lion, visiting his "plenty folks" with the members of the fan who attend our dances. If Frank's unusual ability to do things backed by a willingness to work stay with him, he has a most enviable future before him.

name: Nuttcombe, John William
clubs: Lieutenant and Adjutant First Battalion Staff
description: Here we have the editor-in-chief of "The 1924 Bugle," upon whose shoulders rested the responsibility for a good yearbook, and faithfully he worked to accomplish that end. "Nuts" has always done well in whatever he attempted here at Tech, and he has worked hard in his classes despite a great deal of success during his four-year stay among us. Our chief editor has been prominent in military and social life at V.P.I., holding many duties in both the commandant's office and the social organizations of the corps. His personality has made him a favorite wherever he went and good fellowship abounded, and he has as many friends as anyone could wish. Nuttcombe will pursue the long road of knowledge in higher institutions of learning after he leaves here, and we are sure that he will succeed in whatever else he undertakes.

name: Venable, Bennett Moseley
motto: "Still water runs deep."
clubs: Corporal Company "E", Cadet Club, Lynchburg Club
First Sergeant Company "E", Cadet Club, Lynchburg Club; A. I. E. E., Assistant Manager Football
Captain Company "R", Cadet Club; President of Lynchburg Club; A. I. E. E., Assistant Editor "1924 Eagle"
description: Though Bennett is not water we know he runs deep. It has taken long to listen to him, and yet bottom has not been reached. Generally serious people finally turn to him about things in order that life may be full. And now this is our full-fledged club with a social halo. Someone said the forty-fifth corporal couldn't be a captain, but Bennett tricked the rule for "R" Company's sake. Despite all the good in him we know him as a gentleman first, and always, and the truest friend, no matter what the cost. "His thing, drug that smokes down--I'm freezing."

name: Owens, Emmett Lee
description: Emmett, as he is known to all of us, hails from no other place than "The Garden Spot." He never seems to have any sleep over the late era, but we are inclined to think that he has been keeping something from us. Emmett never had any serious military ambitions, but having a corporalship and urgency thrust upon him, he did the job well. Having disposed of these afflictions, he entered with us this year a full-fledged "Skipper," but the commandant realizing that he was too good a "Skipper" demanded him at Christmas to a lieutenant. Owens is one of the few men in our class who make good grades without "bumping," and fortunate will be the company that lands this budding engineer.

name: Wright, James Elson
motto: "Stop! Look! Listen! Girls, look up your hearts for here comes the Champion heart-breaker of Wicomico. But, he does not confine his conquests to his home town; he conquers the world, just look in the mail box any morning, or glance at the news, he is everywhere."
clubs: Private Company "J" A. I. E. E. Skipper
description: "Jimmy" hails from the village of Wicomico, in the southern part of Gloucester along the shores of the historic York River. Having tried waggoning and farming, he decided that neither agreed with him, and consequently, deserted his plow in the fall of 1929 to become an Electrical Engineer.

name: Peck, Thomas Johnson
description: A good-natured, easy-going, ever-smiling fellow, comes from Corning, Va. It would take endless research to find a man possessing the natural abilities that he has. He is always there with a cheery smile and a "hello." He will put himself out a great deal to do a friend a favor. He has that elusive quality of being able to arrive on his mark without "putting out" much effort. Yes, he is an electrical genius as can readily be understood when the lights go out and the fellows call for lights. May he always light them for dark corners for those with whom he associates in later life. He has worked faithfully for four years to uphold the traditions of the "Skippers," and his work has been rewarded, for many will wonder where another such friend can be found.

name: Rutherford, Donald Hurt
clubs: Sergeant at Arms of Senate Class
description: "Dan" came to us from Bristol and almost immediately jumped into the spotlight as one of the greatest all-around athletes that Tech has yet produced. His record in football, basketball and baseball is most remarkable. In 1924 he led the whole country in drop-kicking; he has twice captained the baseball squad and has played a strong forward on the basketball court for two seasons. That he has other abilities has manifested itself in his academic work, his capacity to form strong friendships, and by the fact that he played the "Skippers" through a "nasty" night at sea. It is with reluctance that we see "Dan" enter professional life because in his leaving we lose a loyal friend who will forever have a place in our hearts.

name: Renn, James Terrell Jr.
motto: "This Jimmie, all right—the natural born 'scribe' of the class—study & sophomore. He has pushed through Physics, Descriptive Geometry, Calculus, etc., with his unlimited knowledge and willing effort. Study! Study! That ain't he. He just knows his berries, that's all. Along with his other A's he always gets a big one on 'Sleepy dog.' Yep! He's heading his class and also the A.S.C.E. We often wonder that he didn't take some hard course like U.K. instead of the 'highest branch of Agriculture.'"

name: Trussel, Emory Harrison
motto: "Emory and Henry"
clubs: Corporal Company "E": Northern Virginia and D.C. Club (1921/22)
Private Company "H": Chemical Club; Track Squad; Fallen Angels (1922/23)
Private Company "H": Chemical Club; Fallen Angels; Skipper (1923/24)
description: As Columbus left behind the mysteries of the Old World to face the unknown ocean, so this young man left behind the dear old farm to face the mysteries of Chemistry at V.P.I. After four long years of sailing through dark, uncharted seas, he has placed his name on the new continent of knowledge in the name Parson's Springs. For this accomplishment he has been decorated with the high and majestic order of "Scutia." With a fresh start under his command, what new lands may not Emory discover in the realms of Chemistry?

name: Ray, James Edward
description: "Teddy" came to us from Fredericksburg, on the banks of the river where George Washington threw the well-known dollar. It is well-proved that "Teddy" is the man who threw it back. A true "Skipper" is Ray, both from a military standpoint and from the manner in which he skillfully pilots his canoe over the waters of his native river. His mastery of this and the other aquatic arts is only exceeded by his ability as a football player. Consistently opposed by men much heavier than himself, he has played his position in a manner that has been a credit to himself and his school. In his four years at A. P. I., "Teddy" has well earned the high place which he holds in the esteem of his fellow students, and if determination and energy, coupled with his winning personality, count for much, we predict for him a most successful career.

name: Rouzie, John Edward
description: We are indeed fortunate in having with us the president, vice-president, secretary, treasurer and only member of Falls' Pistol Club, "Jakie" Rouzie—very much with us, we should say, as "Jakie" even spent the Xmas vacation in Blacksburg, the time allowed not being sufficient for him to make the trip home and return. At the end of "Jakie's" second year of college, he sacrificed his military career and joined the "Toughy fighters," where his greatest ambition was to rival Gabriel. "Jakie's" favorite sport, however, is wrestling, in which he excelled for two years, and was awarded the coveted VP at the end of his Senior year and elected to the position of Captain for the season of 1924/25. His favorite pastime is throwing water from his window on innocent passersby. Many will bear witness to his unerring accuracy.

name: Ryder, William Anthony
description: "Say there, boy, why do they call you 'Satchel'?" We wonder. "Satchel" is a happy-go-lucky fellow with plenty of life and bravery. Many are the ladies of his acquaintances, and those for whom eighteen are supposed to have fallen for his line. Even though several his dates awaited him, we have heard that the woman "Satchel" did not attend the Virginia gang was because her luggage could not be checked on excursion tickets. The summer of '23 found him in training for Uncle Sam's army at Camp Meade. However, "Satchel" soon succumbed to the military atmosphere of camp, and as chief "gimp," his name will live forever in the minds of his associates there. During his stay of six weeks the name of Ryder appeared regularly with the rest on the punishment list with excavation work his specialty.

name: Vertical
motto: "Let's go out and catch a little air."
description: Whenever you hear this expression at a dance, just look around and you will see the light-haired "shik" from Manassas. His military ambitions were neglected, as too much of his time has been taken up by the number of the bar sex. He is full of life and well liked by all of his classmates. He is also considered quite a "scribe" and is always telling us what an awful world this would be without electricity and good electrical engineers. We are expecting in the future a big write-up in some technical magazine, or newspaper about the president of the great Vertical Electric Company.

name: Rolfe, John Thomas
motto: "DUTCHY"
description: Although "Dutchy" has been with us for but a few years, Petersburg still claims him as one of her most valued sons. We see him now as one of that select and privileged band, namely, "Senior Electrical." Once during his career, his military ambitions took the upper hand, and he was forced to wear the white decorations on his sleeve. John's pleasing personality and open heart have won him a place in all our hearts. As a true friend he cannot be excelled; as a hard worker and good student he is hard to beat. It is our belief that this young Electrical Engineer will some day tell the world that his real hobby is electrical engineering. He has made it his life's work to make a fair name both far and wide, but his heart really lies in Petersburg.

name: Wingo, Percy Clamorse
high school: K. F. N. J., PETERSBURG, VA.
motto: "When you are all down and out, remember Jonah came out all right,"
description: Percy is a happy-go-lucky Mechanical Engineer. His favorite expression, which is very typical of this old "Petersburger" who landed in our midst but years ago, because he does not worry over anything and always comes through with flying colors. Of course, it must not be omitted that he is a pronounced success with Earth's latest creatures, and we hope that little Dan will soon have his works completed. After graduation "P. C." expects to put aside his pen for calipers and compass, to lay away his Senior cap and don overalls and begin his upward climb to success as a Mechanical Engineer. Knowing Percy as we do, we hope that he will not stop short of some great adventure (perpetual motion, maybe) which is his greatest ideal.

name: Samueck, George Alexander
description: George came from Russia to Canada while only a small boy. There he learned to love the lakes and the forests, acquiring an enviable love of hunting, trapping, fishing, and other outdoor sports peculiar to that country. In 1917 George, having moved to Lynchburg, Va., heard the call and immediately enlisted in the American Army. After the Armistice he returned home and from there came to Lynchburg to pursue his education. If you don't believe he knows how to "handle" his problems, take a look at some of his test papers yourself. He is not afraid of work, and when there is a hard task to perform, simply turn it over to George and "Let George Do It."

name: Shepherd, Carroll Wise
description: "Shep" is studying the art of "grease wiping" and he is aiming to invent some new process that would eliminate the grease and the wiping from the profession. During summer months he has applied his theory to actual railroad work and from all reports he has been doing well. Some day we hope that he will take charge of the "Huckleberry Limited" ("Limited" meaning 10 miles per hour and a half), and underwrite the speed of the "snorting link" between Blackburg and the outside world. "Shep" is never idle. When you cannot find him in his room working some math problems or writing letters to "her," you may be sure that he is at a meeting of the local Masonic Lodge. Best of all, he is a good pal and can always be depended on.

name: Somerville, Frank Merrifield
clubs: Vice President A. S. A. E. (1922-23)
description: Here is Frank who hails from Staunton, but we do not hold this against him. We wonder if his oratorical talents are typical of his methods. Such gestures? Perhaps he will be an exception or a stump speaker some day. Although our efforts to learn his relations with the fair sex have been in vain, we notice that he seems to be open to attack when Kentucky is mentioned. Frank wears a P, which he will carry on the football diamond as pitcher. This, however, is only one of his virtues and seems to make it impossible to enumerate them. And now, as we reach the conclusion of our sketch, we must ask ourselves the long, long trail that winds into the light of our dreams. There is a heartfelt feeling in our hearts as we say to him, "Goodbye."

name: Simpson, William Frederick
motto: "And this is 'Simp,' the boy who hails from 'Ole Kentucky,' the land of 'beautiful women' and 'bad boys.' We do not know his ideas concerning the 'bad boys,' but those concerning the 'beautiful women' are obvious to all who know him well. At the beginning of his senior year his affections were balanced between two; but now, the balances seem to be pointing towards Hallie. However, Simpson is one who has never turned his back, but has marched straight forward. He has gone through his four years at Tech with all the straightforward courage that is implied with a Scotch humor and a fine spirit that has made its influence felt in all that he has done and on all with whom he has come in contact."

name: Stepp, William Knight
motto: "Judas Priest! How do they expect a fellow to get to a formation on time when they haven't got bells in the barracks!"
clubs: Private Company "E," A. I. E. E.
description: This was the subject of Willie's first clash with the C. O., but the result of this and subsequent engagements caused him to change his original intention of majoring in military. He tries to convince us of his indifference toward the "homies," but from what we have heard from various outside sources we are inclined to doubt him and think that he believes in the maxim, "there is safety in numbers." Willie took a great step forward when he enrolled in E. E. at V. P. I. and he will without a doubt make a success in the sales end of the electrical industry.

name: Young, Andrew Jackson
motto: "Every house alarm, I'm wake."
clubs: Private Company "E" A. L. E. E. Fallen Angels. Richmond Club
Lieutenant Company "E" A. L. E. E. Company First Ball; Richmond Club
description: Anyone that ever tried to order "Jack" to quit the "Batteries" has heard this. Nothing short of a 100 would stir "Jack" from his slumber, as the alarm clock, hanging by his bed, has absolutely no effect. In addition to this, "Jack" is a literary critic of sorts and can tell anyone the best magazines of the day. "Jack" is one of the best natural talents we have, and had the pleasure of knowing and he will go to any extreme to keep a friend. Needless to say, he has a heart of stone. Up to Christmas this year, worry was the harbinger of things from this boy's mind; but since then, something besides A. L. E. E. seems to occupy his thoughts. We haven't found out his secret yet, but we have our suspicions.

In addition to this, "Jack" is a literary critic of sorts and can tell anyone the best magazines of the day. "Jack" is one of the best natural talents we have, and had the pleasure of knowing and he will go to any extreme to keep a friend. Needless to say, he has a heart of stone. Up to Christmas this year, worry was the harbinger of things from this boy's mind; but since then, something besides A. L. E. E. seems to occupy his thoughts. We haven't found out his secret yet, but we have our suspicions.

name: Stull, Charles Meade
description: Rev J---"Un, un, un, the rest of the class keep quiet and listen to Mr. Stull." Stull---"Well---er, I did get a chance to study this business last night." We wonder what? It would take a probability to find the reason. Music came to us from the Metropolitan Academy marked him as a man of ability. During his stay at Tech he has tried football; but on account of injuries received in prep school he was unsuccessful. Next to be part of a newspaper, he went out to manage track and make it. We are sure that he would have made the Phi Kappa Psi had he studied material.

name: Sanders, William Sabiston
description: The boy who hails from North Carolina and is proud of it, has come to be a well known figure on the V.P.I. campus. We attribute "Bill's" popularity to his ready wit, general humor, originality, and faithfulness as a friend. His success has been complete at V.P.I. aside from his host of friends. "Bill" as captain and manager of the wrestling team, will show us some good work on the mat. He is one of the few who, for four years went down on Mike's Field and helped whip the big team into shape, taking his knocks as they came and coming up with a smile. "Bill" is also a good talker and has held the corps in delightful suspense on many a timely subject. At one time he almost became the corps's triumphant hero held at Blacksburg and moved to North Carolina for the semester.

name: Kraemer, Karl Edward
description: "Hey, 'IKE,' how are you?" is the usual familiar greeting in the vicinity of Fourth Division. "IKE" is one of those men who seems to be everywhere, radiating amiability and good cheer; hence, he is always in demand when good fellows get together. Military holds no terrors for "IKE." Having mastered the science of squads right and left in two short years, he transferred to the "Band," where he at last found the seven harmonies with such results that he now makes the orchestra do everything but yodel. "IKE's" chosen field is Mechanical Engineering, and we look forward to the day that we crawl under the steering wheel of a KRAEMER, the last word in automotive design.

name: Hyatt, Gilkison Johnson
description: Here we have Hyatt Johnson, but known to all on the campus as "Box Car." Why he should be called by this unusual name is a mystery to most of us. "Box Car" is an all around man in every sense of the word. Chess, tennis, golf, and the intricacies of Bridge hold no terrors for him. Football and wrestling are also among his accomplishments. With the saxophone he is right at home, being almost able to play anything that is written, and some that is not. "Box Car" is bound to make good when he gets out of college. He has already proved that he can get along without the support of others and that he is in every way qualified for any position that he may take.

name: Watkins, Cornelius Tyree
motto: "TYREE"
description: C. Tyree, Jr., if you don't see him look for him for he is worth seeing and hearing for when he speaks he has something to say. He hails from the capital city, is a product of J. M. H. S. and has ambitions, aspirations, and ideals which are worthy of his Alma Mater. Ambitions along what lines? Academic, military, matters pertaining to love and the other great things which enter into life; what else could one feel or desire? He is a hard worker who never gives up, and because of this his career at Tech has been a success and will continue a success throughout life.

name: Beazley, William Raymond
description: Introducing "RED," dean of the I. E.'s. He says that I. E. stands for Independent Engineer; whereas, in reality it is the abbreviation for Industrial Education, the name of the course that he is taking. In one sense of the word he is the highest one of the Seniors taking this course, and this is in the color sense, his hair being the medium. While not generally recognized as a scholar, he seems to have very little difficulty in making good grades on all his subjects and it is not an uncommon occurrence on the night before a test to find several of his classmates in his room seeking information on any of the very interesting subjects to which the I. E.'s are exposed.

name: Smith, Wake Brackett
motto: "Yes, there are thousands upon thousands of Smiths, but there is only one Wake Brackett 'Bobo' Smith in all the world. Furthermore, he is a full-blooded, unadulterated, yard wide and high-toned 'Skipper,' which fact places him above the ordinary realm of mere men. Wake has no claims to athletic honors, but his high military achievements, but he has served his time faithfully as a cadet private of the first water. His chivalry, skill and ready wit have helped him while away many a weary hour. From the standpoint of scientific analysis, we would say that Wake has many capabilities and great possibilities."

name: Jones, John Marshall
clubs: Staff, The Virginia Tech, Assistant Manager Baseball Squad
Assistant Athletic Editor, The Virginia Tech
description: A string of avocations which leave no doubt as to the speaker's idea about getting up before nine-thirty, was closely followed from the last by something over six feet of Jones. But don't get the idea that "Jack" is lazy unless you can call a person lazy who takes Mining Engineering, instructs "Rats" in the gentle art of dancing, carries on a complete correspondence with "Everybody" in Lynchburg and still finds time enough to do considerable "Bugle" work. Anyone who has the energy and determination of Jack, and who hates sleep as he does, is bound to succeed in whatever he undertakes in life.

name: Tinsley, Albert Craig
description: Happily born under Scorpio of the Zodiac, "AL" has the wondrous faculty of never worrying about the morrow, and yet, for him, that elusive day is always taken care of. He has the happy popularity of being able, with the least amount of exertion, to get his work done without the usual rush. This lad from the quiet banks of the James is not without the frivolous phase of life even though he may at most times seem dignified. Even in his High School days he was properly labeled "Jane Baby" and never since has he allowed the fair Goddess Terpsichore to forsake him whenever a chance to sing a wicked show presented itself. Take good care of that transom, "AL," we are all confident that the Civil Engineering World will hear from you.

name: Baker, Joseph Julian
description: After completing two years at Tech, Julian spent a year in the State of Washington. The following year he returned to V.P.I. He became a member of our class, and has risen to many responsible positions in the student body. As business manager of the 1924 "Bugle", he has demonstrated his ability as a business man. We are indebted to him for the best "Bugle" ever given, and also for those given to the First Division. Under his efficient management, the Virginia Tech Mascot has enjoyed the most successful season in their history. Julian is a rare combination of good humor and hard work; a man to be looked for by his personality and admired for his ability. To say that he attracted the love of his heart caused many a broken heart would be putting it mildly, for he is a woman's man, "anyhow."

name: Brown, George Ernest
description: And now we shall introduce, "Ernie" Brown, the Commercial wizard. Not only is he a Commercial shark, but he is a chary leader of no mean ability. Although his school life has been filled with various activities, military, puritanical, athletic, etc., he has always found time to keep up in his classes with high standing, and has shown much interest in the social life on the campus. "Ernie" showed during his Sophomore and Junior years that he could be a military man if necessary, and then he responded to the call of the "S. P.S." and has proved a worthy member. When "Ernie" goes out to take his place in the world, whether it be in Roanoke or in Baltimore, we feel sure that he will succeed and meet with approval.

name: Carroll, Edward Chester
description: "Pat" hails from Richmond, coming to V.P.I. with a reputation as a basketball player, which he has upheld, being a star forward for four years and an equally capable baseball player. He has many friends at Tech and his popularity as a clean athlete and good fellow is shown by the fact that he was captain of the 1924 basketball team and president of the Athletic Association. As our space is limited we cannot go into details about his affairs with the fair sex, but must say that he has the reputation of being a "lady killer." "Pat" chose Commercial Engineering as his profession, and if he plays that game as he did those at Tech, nothing but success lies ahead.
